metadata.from: State of Iowa, Department of Public Safety (official document); operator is James E. Hanifen (Sr., based on birth year ~1890s visible and occupation 'Shop Proprietier')
metadata.to: James E. Hanifen / James Edward Hanifen
metadata.location_written_from: Des Moines, Iowa (State of Iowa Dept. of Public Safety)
metadata.envelope_notes: This is not a letter but rather a 1939-1941 Iowa Operator's License (driver's license) document set belonging to James E. Hanifen Sr. It includes: the license card itself (inside), a renewal temporary permit (front), and what appears to be a partial letter or notice overlapping the inside image regarding the Temporary Permit. The operator's number is 4455. The license expires July 5, 1941. County: Polk. Commissioner of Public Safety: Karl W. Fischer.
metadata.mood_tone: Official/administrative document; neutral governmental tone. The partial overlapping letter fragment discusses the Temporary Permit being honored for Iowa residents.
metadata.legibility_notes: The inside license card is fairly legible. The front temporary permit is handwritten in cursive and partially legible. A separate paper fragment is overlapping the inside image, partially obscuring the license details and readable only in part. Birth year partially visible as '189[?]'. The overlapping note mentions 'This Temporary Permit actually... 1939-41 license, and will be honored... the United States... Iowa residents... is an immense amount... renewal licenses to our... before the reissuance... September 1st... have not received... Please d[o]... tember... in th[e]...'
metadata.historical_context: This is a WWII-era Iowa driver's license (1939-1941) with a renewal temporary permit dated June 20, 1941. The overlapping note fragment references reissuance difficulties possibly related to wartime administrative backlogs.

pages.transcription:
OPERATOR'S LICENSE  No.
State of Iowa, Dept. of Public Safety

NOTICE. This stub to be torn off by the court and mailed to Operator's License Div., Dept. of Public Safety, if convicted of a motor vehicle law violation, as provided in Sec. 238, Chapter 134.

FIRST OFFENSE

--- FOLD HERE ---

STATE OF IOWA
1939-1941
OPERATOR'S No.  4455

Applicant's picture will appear on his [license]

---

The Department of Public Safety of the State of Iowa hereby acknowledges receipt from  James Edward Hanifen
of application and Fifty Cents (50c) fee for renewal of 1939-41 Iowa Operator's License Number  4455  which expires July 5, 1941, and hereby extends to said applicant permission to operate a motor vehicle while the department is completing its investigation and determination of all facts relative to such applicant's right to receive an operator's license, subject however to any restrictions imposed on the above numbered 1939-41 license. For the purpose of restrictions and identification said 1939-41 license shall be considered a part hereof and at all times must be kept herewith.

This temporary permit is issued under authority of Section 5013.07, Code of Iowa, 1939, and must be in the immediate possession of said applicant while operating a motor vehicle after July 5, 1941, until receipt of new license. It shall be invalid when such applicant's renewal license has been issued or for good cause has been refused, and in any event shall be invalid after September 15, 1941.

KARL W. FISCHER
COMMISSIONER OF PUBLIC SAFETY

Date  6-20-41

County  Polk        By  [illegible signature]
                         Department Representative

(Over)

summary: This document (letter ID 83) is not a personal letter but rather the 1939-1941 Iowa Operator's License (driver's license) belonging to James E. Hanifen Sr., father of Bob, Jim, and John Hanifen. The license shows he lived at 424 Virginia, Des Moines, Iowa, and was a 'Shop Proprietier' [sic], approximately 5'9", 175 lbs, with light hair and gray eyes. Accompanying the license is a temporary renewal permit dated June 20, 1941, signed by Commissioner of Public Safety Karl W. Fischer, extending his driving privileges through September 15, 1941, while his new license was being processed. A partially legible note fragment overlaps the inside image, apparently discussing the Temporary Permit's validity for Iowa residents.
